title: Upanisā Sutta
ontology_guardrail: do not reify path variables as substances
summary: Positive dependent arising from suffering through confidence, gladness, joy, calm, concentration, clear seeing, disenchantment, dispassion, and liberation.

title: Vimutti Sutta
ontology_guardrail: do not reduce liberation to calm or intellectual view alone
summary: Freedom requires both affective release from passion and wisdom release from ignorance.

title: Vimuttāyatana Sutta
ontology_guardrail: do not treat content modes as substitutes for direct practice
summary: Hearing, teaching, reciting, reflecting, and attending to stillness can each open the joy-calm-concentration-release chain.

title: Anattalakkhaṇa Sutta
ontology_guardrail: not-self is non-ownership, not nihilistic self-hatred
summary: The aggregates cannot be controlled as self; what is afflicted and not fully commandable is not fit to be owned as mine/I/self.

title: Anattā Sutta
ontology_guardrail: do not turn non-ownership into dissociation from embodied life
summary: The five aggregates are not-self; de-owning them reduces appropriation and supports release.

title: Nibbāna Sutta
ontology_guardrail: do not define nibbana as a measurable physics object or annihilation
summary: Nibbāna is framed as unborn, unbecome, unmade, unconditioned; for ToL it functions as the end of compulsory conditioning and support.

title: Ānāpānasati Sutta
ontology_guardrail: breath practice is a training interface, not a metaphysical warrant
summary: Mindfulness of breathing fulfills the establishments of mindfulness, awakening factors, and knowledge/release.

title: Āneñjasappāya Sutta
ontology_guardrail: do not confuse refined stability with irreversible freedom
summary: Even refined equanimous or formless states can become bondage if relished, welcomed, or fastened to.
